story.asA: developer integrating Recipe into automated workflows
story.iWant: machine-readable JSON output with the --json flag
story.soThat: I can parse conversion results programmatically in scripts, CI/CD pipelines, and other automation tools
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create Output Formatting Infrastructure (AC-1, AC-2) - Create `cmd/cli/output.go` file (or extend if Story 3-5 created it) - Define `ConversionResult` struct with JSON tags (input, output, source_format, target_format, success, duration_ms, file_size_bytes, warnings, error) - Define `BatchResult` struct with JSON tags (batch, total, success_count, error_count, duration_ms, results array) - Implement `formatResult(result ConversionResult, jsonMode bool) string` function - Implement `formatBatchResult(result BatchResult, jsonMode bool) string` function
### Task 2: Add JSON Flag to Root Command (AC-1) - Open `cmd/cli/root.go` - Add persistent flag: `rootCmd.PersistentFlags().Bool("json", false, "Output results as JSON")` - Create helper function `isJSONMode(cmd *cobra.Command) bool` to check flag value
### Task 3: Integrate JSON Output in Convert Command (AC-2, AC-3, AC-7) - Open `cmd/cli/convert.go` - Modify convert command to collect result data (input, output, formats, success, duration, file size, warnings, errors) - Implement `outputJSON(result ConversionResult)` function marshaling to stdout - Implement `outputHumanReadable(result ConversionResult)` function for normal mode - Ensure JSON goes to stdout, human-readable success to stdout, errors to stderr
### Task 4: Integrate JSON Output in Batch Command (AC-4, AC-7) - Open `cmd/cli/batch.go` - Modify batch command to collect per-file results - Build BatchResult struct with summary counts and results array - Implement `outputBatchJSON(result BatchResult)` function - Ensure proper stream routing (JSON to stdout, verbose logs to stderr)
### Task 5: Ensure Stdout/Stderr Separation (AC-7) - Review all output statements in convert.go and batch.go - Verify JSON always goes to os.Stdout - Verify verbose logs always go to os.Stderr - Verify human-readable messages use correct streams - Test combined --json --verbose flags
### Task 6: Add Unit Tests - Create `cmd/cli/output_test.go` - Test ConversionResult JSON marshaling - Test BatchResult JSON marshaling - Test omitempty fields (warnings, error, file_size_bytes)
### Task 7: Add Integration Tests (AC-5, AC-6) - Test single conversion JSON output - Test batch conversion JSON output - Test error conversion JSON output - Test jq compatibility (if jq installed) - Test Python compatibility (if Python installed)
### Task 8: Add JSON + Verbose Integration Test (AC-7) - Test JSON output with verbose flag - Verify stdout contains only JSON - Verify stderr contains verbose logs - Verify no stream mixing
### Task 9: Update Documentation - Update README.md with JSON output examples - Document JSON schema - Add scripting examples (jq, Python)
### Task 10: Performance Testing - Measure JSON marshaling overhead - Verify JSON output overhead less than 10ms

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: JSON Flag Configuration - `--json` flag enables JSON output mode - Flag defined in root command (global flag available to all commands) - Flag accessible via Cobra context in all commands - Boolean flag (no value required: presence = enabled) - Defaults to false (normal human-readable output) - Mutually exclusive with verbose mode behavior (JSON takes precedence for stdout)
### AC-2: JSON Output Structure for Single Conversion - Valid JSON object written to stdout - Required fields: input, output, source_format, target_format, success, duration_ms - Optional fields: file_size_bytes, warnings, error - No human-readable text mixed with JSON (all logs to stderr if verbose also enabled)
### AC-3: JSON Output for Failed Conversions - Failed conversions still output valid JSON - success field set to false - error field contains error message - Exit code still non-zero (preserves shell scripting compatibility)
### AC-4: JSON Output for Batch Operations - Batch operations output single JSON object with array of results - Required fields: batch, total, success_count, error_count, duration_ms, results - Each result in results array follows single conversion schema (AC-2)
### AC-5: jq Compatibility - JSON output parsable by jq without errors - Common jq queries work correctly - Field names use snake_case (jq convention)
### AC-6: Python json Module Compatibility - JSON output parsable by Python's json module without errors - Field types map correctly to Python types (str, bool, int, list)
### AC-7: JSON + Verbose Mode Interaction - When both --json and --verbose flags present, JSON goes to stdout, logs go to stderr - Stdout contains ONLY valid JSON (no log messages mixed in) - Stderr contains verbose logs as normal

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: JSON output flag provides machine-readable structured data. Valid JSON to stdout with fields: input, output, source_format, target_format, success, duration_ms, file_size_bytes, warnings, error. Batch mode outputs single JSON object with results array. Compatible with jq and Python json module.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: ConversionResult struct wraps output bytes + metadata for display. Fields: InputFile string, OutputFile string, SourceFormat string, TargetFormat string, Success bool, Error string, Duration time.Duration, FileSize int64, Warnings []string. JSON tags use snake_case.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Requirement: Machine-readable output for scripting with --json flag. Format includes input, output, source/target formats, success boolean, duration, file size, warnings array. Acceptance: Useful for CI/CD and automation, parsable by jq and Python, doesn't clutter normal output.

doc.title: Epic 3 Technical Specification - CLI Interface
doc.snippet: JSON output formatting: less than 10ms additional overhead. Normal output: less than 5ms formatting time. Verbose logging: less than 15% performance impact.

artifact.reason: Core conversion function. JSON output will capture its results including timing, success/failure, and any warnings.
Error type that JSON output will serialize in the error field when conversion fails.
Logger from Story 3-5. Must coordinate with JSON mode - logs to stderr when --json --verbose combined.

package.name: encoding/json
package.reason: JSON marshaling and unmarshaling for structured output

package.name: github.com/spf13/cobra
package.reason: CLI framework - will be used to add persistent --json flag to root command

story-context.constraints: - Story 3-1 (Cobra CLI Structure) must be complete to provide root command for --json flag - Story 3-2 (Convert Command) must exist for single conversion JSON output - Story 3-3 (Batch Processing) must exist for batch JSON output - Story 3-5 (Verbose Logging) coordinates with this story on output.go module and stream routing - JSON output MUST go to stdout (never stderr) to enable piping - Verbose logs MUST go to stderr (never stdout) when combined with --json - Exit codes MUST be preserved (JSON mode doesn't change shell scripting behavior) - Field names MUST use snake_case (jq/Python convention, not Go camelCase) - JSON MUST be valid and parsable by standard tools (jq, Python json module) - Optional fields MUST use omitempty to keep JSON clean - JSON marshaling overhead MUST be less than 10ms (NFR requirement) - Recommended implementation order: Story 3-5 first, then 3-6 (to avoid output.go merge conflicts)

interface.name: ConversionResult
interface.signature: type ConversionResult struct { Input string `json:"input"`; Output string `json:"output"`; SourceFormat string `json:"source_format"`; TargetFormat string `json:"target_format"`; Success bool `json:"success"`; DurationMs int64 `json:"duration_ms"`; FileSizeBytes int64 `json:"file_size_bytes,omitempty"`; Warnings []string `json:"warnings,omitempty"`; Error string `json:"error,omitempty"` }
interface.description: Data structure for single conversion result. Marshals to JSON with snake_case field names. Optional fields use omitempty.

interface.name: BatchResult
interface.description: Data structure for batch operation results. Contains summary counts and array of per-file results.

interface.name: formatResult
interface.signature: func formatResult(result ConversionResult, jsonMode bool) string
interface.description: Formats conversion result as JSON (if jsonMode=true) or human-readable text (if jsonMode=false). Returns string to write to stdout.

interface.name: formatBatchResult
interface.signature: func formatBatchResult(result BatchResult, jsonMode bool) string
interface.description: Formats batch result as JSON (if jsonMode=true) or human-readable summary (if jsonMode=false). Returns string to write to stdout.

interface.name: isJSONMode
interface.description: Helper function to check if --json flag is enabled from Cobra command context.

interface.name: json.Marshal
interface.description: Standard library function for marshaling Go structs to JSON bytes.

interface.name: json.MarshalIndent
interface.signature: func json.MarshalIndent(v any, prefix, indent string) ([]byte, error)
interface.description: Marshals to JSON with pretty-printing (indentation). Use for human-readable JSON output.

tests.standards: This story follows the Recipe project testing standards established in Epic 1: - Table-driven tests for multiple scenarios - Use of testdata/ directory for sample files (1,501 files available) - Unit tests for JSON marshaling (ConversionResult, BatchResult structs) - Integration tests executing CLI as subprocess via os/exec - Capture and parse stdout to verify JSON validity - Test compatibility with external tools (jq, Python) if available - All tests use Go stdlib testing package (no external test frameworks) - Coverage goal: greater than 90% for new CLI output code
tests.locations: cmd/cli/output_test.go - Unit tests for JSON marshaling and formatting cmd/cli/convert_test.go - Integration tests for single conversion JSON output cmd/cli/batch_test.go - Integration tests for batch conversion JSON output
tests.ideas:
### Unit Test Ideas (AC-1, AC-2): - TestConversionResultJSON_Success: Marshal successful result, verify all required fields present - TestConversionResultJSON_WithWarnings: Verify warnings array included when present - TestConversionResultJSON_Error: Marshal failed result, verify error field and success=false - TestConversionResultJSON_OmitEmpty: Verify optional fields omitted when empty - TestBatchResultJSON: Marshal batch result, verify summary counts and results array - TestJSONFieldNames: Verify snake_case field names (not camelCase)
### Integration Test Ideas (AC-2, AC-3, AC-4): - TestConvertJSONOutput_SingleFile: Run conversion with --json, parse stdout, verify all fields - TestConvertJSONOutput_ValidJSON: Verify json.Unmarshal succeeds on stdout - TestConvertJSONOutput_ErrorCase: Test conversion failure outputs valid JSON with success=false - TestBatchJSONOutput: Run batch with --json, verify batch structure and results array - TestBatchJSONOutput_MixedResults: Batch with some successes and failures, verify counts accurate
### Integration Test Ideas (AC-5): - TestJQCompatibility: Pipe JSON output to jq, verify parsing succeeds - TestJQQueries: Test common jq queries (.success, .results[], .warnings | length) - Skip if jq not installed in test environment
### Integration Test Ideas (AC-6): - TestPythonJSONCompatibility: Run Python script to parse JSON output - TestPythonFieldTypes: Verify Python sees correct types (bool, int, str, list) - Skip if Python not installed in test environment
### Integration Test Ideas (AC-7): - TestJSONWithVerbose: Run with --json --verbose, verify stdout=JSON and stderr=logs - TestJSONWithVerbose_NoMixing: Verify no log messages in stdout - TestJSONWithVerbose_VerboseLogsPresent: Verify stderr contains DEBUG messages
### Performance Test Ideas: - BenchmarkJSONMarshal: Measure JSON marshaling time for ConversionResult - BenchmarkJSONMarshal_Batch: Measure marshaling time for BatchResult with 100 results - Verify less than 10ms overhead per NFR requirement
